[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [43°22′54″N 87°56′18″W﻿ / ﻿43.38167°N 87.93833°W﻿ / 43.38167; -87.93833](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Payne_Hotel&params=43_22_54_N_87_56_18_W_type:landmark_region:US-WI)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

United States historic place

| [Payne Hotel is located in Wisconsin](/wiki/File:USA_Wisconsin_location_map.svg "Payne Hotel is located in Wisconsin") Payne Hotel   Show map of Wisconsin  [Payne Hotel is located in the United States](/wiki/File:Usa_edcp_location_map.svg "Payne Hotel is located in the United States") Payne Hotel   Show map of the United States | |
| Location | 310 E. Green Bay Ave., [Saukville](/wiki/Saukville,_Wisconsin "Saukville, Wisconsin"), [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin") |
| Coordinates | [43°22′54″N 87°56′18″W﻿ / ﻿43.38167°N 87.93833°W﻿ / 43.38167; -87.93833](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Payne_Hotel&params=43_22_54_N_87_56_18_W_type:landmark_region:US-WI) |

| Added to NRHP | March 14, 1991 |

The **Payne Hotel** is a former [stagecoach](/wiki/Stagecoach "Stagecoach") inn located along [Highway 33](/wiki/Highway_33 "Highway 33") in [Saukville, Wisconsin](/wiki/Saukville,_Wisconsin "Saukville, Wisconsin"). Deemed worthy of preservation, the hotel has been listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") since March 14, 1991.[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

William Payne was born in [London](/wiki/London "London") in 1806 and immigrated to the U.S. in 1826. In 1846 he founded Saukville, building a [sawmill](/wiki/Sawmill "Sawmill") and [gristmill](/wiki/Gristmill "Gristmill") on the [Milwaukee River](/wiki/Milwaukee_River "Milwaukee River"). In 1848 he built the Payne Hotel where the old Dekorra Military Road met the old [Green Bay Trail](/wiki/Green_Bay_Trail "Green Bay Trail"). It served as a [stagecoach](/wiki/Stagecoach "Stagecoach") stop for people traveling between Chicago/Milwaukee and Green Bay.[[3]](#cite_note-nom-3)

The hotel is a 2.5-story wood-frame building. It is in [Greek Revival](/wiki/Greek_Revival "Greek Revival") style, with a low-pitched roof, [cornice](/wiki/Cornice "Cornice") returns, and symmetric windows and chimneys. Along with serving travelers, it hosted dances for locals in the ballroom on the third floor. It may well have hosted local meetings too, since Payne served as a [Town Supervisor](/wiki/Town_Supervisor "Town Supervisor"), Commissioner of Highways, and [Justice of the Peace](/wiki/Justice_of_the_Peace "Justice of the Peace").[[3]](#cite_note-nom-3)
